qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Image decompression bomb causes denial of service via unbounded memory allocation in GD thumbnail processing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding reports GD thumbnail memory exhaustion from large image dimensions; it does not identify the creation-time blueprint injection path or authorization bypass for disabled page, file, or user create permissions.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory concerns authenticated authorization bypass in Kirby page, file, and user creation via dynamic blueprint configuration injection, where an injected blueprint property can override disabled create permissions. The qualifying finding instead describes a GD image decompression denial of service.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-6gqr-mx34-wh8r states that Kirby creation requests for pages, files, and users failed to filter the blueprint property, allowing injected options such as create=true to override configured create permissions.

image_decompression_bomb_dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies GdLib::process, Darkroom thumbnail processing, and SimpleImage::fromFile memory allocation as the vulnerable path, not a permission-check or blueprint-injection flaw.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matched the target advisory root cause, so runtime PoC execution was not applicable.
